twitter_thread: Guy Eisenkot's Baz just raised $9 million, 3x Bridgecrew's initial $3 million round.
Baz automates 80% of code reviews on GitHub and GitLab, using 12k+ lines of custom code.
Eisenkot's team trains AI models with PyTorch and TensorFlow, processing 500k+ code commits daily.
They're targeting dev teams with 10-50 members, where manual code review costs $150k+ per year.
Baz's approach reduces coding time by 30%, focusing on Java, Python, and JavaScript projects.
With $9 million, Baz is valued at $40 million, a 4x increase from its seed round.
What's the most painful coding task you've automated, and how much time did it save? #aicoding #devtools
pack.linkedin: Guy Eisenkot's Baz has raised $9 million to automate code reviews on GitHub and GitLab, using a custom stack with PyTorch and TensorFlow. This approach processes 500k+ code commits daily and reduces coding time by 30% for Java, Python, and JavaScript projects. Targeting dev teams with 10-50 members, Baz aims to save $150k+ per year in manual code review costs. With a valuation of $40 million, Baz is taking a significant step forward. What's the most challenging coding task you've had to automate in your own workflow, and how did you approach it?
tiktok_hooks: Guy Eisenkot's $9 million bet: can AI really automate 80% of code reviews?
12k lines of custom code: the secret to Baz's AI-powered coding efficiency?
Baz: the $40 million startup revolutionizing code reviews with PyTorch and TensorFlow
pack.reddit_headline: Guy Eisenkot's Baz: can AI-powered code reviews really save dev teams $150k+ per year?
